definition: The Fed is simultaneously constrained from easing by above-target inflation (core PCE ~2.6-2.8%) and constrained from tightening by fiscal/financial fragility. The Taylor Rule prescribes 75-150bp above the current stance, meaning the Fed is already quasi-accommodative. The March 2020 emergency QE playbook is unavailable in an inflationary context.
evidence: Taylor Rule prescribes 75-150bp above current stance
0/7 base rate for successful consolidation without 200bp+ monetary offset
Even 150bp of cuts leave all-in refinancing costs 200-300bp above original issuance for sub-IG
No curve path resolves both monetary and fiscal signals simultaneously
Six agents converge from different frameworks
open_questions: Under what conditions could the Fed credibly ease without reigniting inflation expectations?
Is there a non-traditional policy tool that resolves the trap without full QE?
